steps.title: No real solutions requires negative discriminant
steps.content: When a quadratic has no real solutions, the discriminant is negative. For $-x^2 + bx - 49 = 0$, identify $a = -1$, the coefficient of $x$ is $b$, and $c = -49$. We need the discriminant to be less than zero:

steps.title: Solve the inequality for the least integer b
steps.content:
Substitute the values:
$$b^2 - 4(-1)(-49) < 0$$
$$b^2 - 196 < 0$$
$$b^2 < 196$$
$$|b| < 14$$
This gives $-14 < b < 14$. Since $b$ is an integer, the possible values are $\{-13, -12, ..., 12, 13\}$. The least value of $b$ is −13.
